primary_top5.debate_reasoning: The bull case has solid technical evidence (stacked rising MAs, elevated volume, proximity to highs) and supportive options skew, but it lacks a confirmed breakout and any identifiable near-term catalyst to justify a high-probability +10% move in 7 sessions. The bear case is strengthened by the failed/absent Donchian breakout signal, near-overbought conditions, and fundamental leverage/FCF fragility that can quickly punish a momentum stall, making the near-term risk/reward less asymmetric than the original 10/10 implies.
key_risks_from_debate: Breakout failure near 52-week highs leading to mean reversion toward short-term moving averages within a week
Crowded/expensive options positioning (very high call/put, high IV) flipping from supportive to suppressive if spot stalls or dips
Balance-sheet and cash-flow sensitivity (high net debt/EBITDA, negative FCF metrics) amplifying drawdowns on any risk-off tape
